verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II single-pass TM glycoprotein with its N-terminal hydrophobic segment acting as a permanent TM anchor. Although the dominant Golgi-resident form catalyzes glycoconjugate synthesis, multiple sources (UniProt P15291, PubMed:1714903) confirm that the long/processed isoform is 'mainly localized at the plasma membrane' where it functions as a cell-surface recognition molecule during cell–cell and cell–matrix interactions (e.g., fertilization, development). The protein is listed under Cell membrane (single-pass type II) and Cell surface in subcellular localization databases. This constitutes stable, anchor-mediated outer-leaflet display, qualifying as a classical surface protein.
